title: arrhenatherum elatius subsp. erianthum
intro: Arrhenatherum elatius subsp. erianthum, also known as meadow oat-grass or false oat-grass, is a subspecies of perennial grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Arrhenatherum elatius subsp. erianthum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically proven.
